response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense-funded computer networking research, with its precursor ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969 via a UCLA-to-Stanford Research Institute message; a 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols laid the modern internet’s technical foundation, while Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 World Wide Web invention (with HTML, URLs, and HTTP) and 1993’s Mosaic browser made web access available to non-expert users. The late 1990s brought a massive dot-com investment boom that collapsed in a 2001 crash, though resilient firms like Amazon and Google grew into some of history’s most valuable companies; subsequent 2000s innovations included 2006’s AWS launch (pioneering cloud computing), 2007’s iPhone (fueling ubiquitous mobile internet), and mid-decade social media platforms like Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube that redefined global communication. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people worldwide and underpins virtually every facet of modern life, from commerce and communication to entertainment.
